vulnerability.reason: trust in LLM-based Q&A captures automation bias, and susceptibility to plausible falsehoods mirrors accepting answers as fact
vulnerability.reference: Users accept plausible-sounding answers as fact due to automation bias.
vulnerability.generated: Trusting LLM-based Q&A, users lack the expertise to identify plausible falsehoods, rendering them susceptible to misinformation.

impact.reason: widespread misinformation is explicitly stated, matching the spread of false answers from the reference
impact.reference: Misinformation spreads when false answers are shared without detection.
impact.generated: Users acting on false information leads to flawed decisions, widespread misinformation, and erosion of public trust in AI-assisted Q&A systems.

dose_response.reason: causal chain from task misalignment through user trust in plausible falsehoods to misinformation and flawed decisions is preserved, with only additional details added
dose_response.reference: Task misalignment from hypernetwork-generated adapters causes plausible-sounding incorrect answers in QA systems, which general users trust as fact, resulting in misinformation spread and harmful high-stakes decisions.
dose_response.generated: Hypernetwork-generated LoRA adapters inducing task misalignment and distributional shift, through general users trusting LLM-based Q&A, triggers their susceptibility to plausible falsehoods, leading to users acting on false information, flawed decisions, widespread misinformation, and eroded public trust in AI-assisted Q&A systems.
